title: Fixing Invalid Git Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation

statement.markdown: The `validateCherryPickHash` function checks if a given Git commit hash is valid for cherry-picking. However, it has a bug that allows invalid hashes to pass the validation. Fix the function to correctly validate the hash and return `true` only if it's a valid commit hash. The function should handle both short and long commit hashes.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a Git commit hash, either short (7 characters) or long (40 characters).
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the commit hash is valid for cherry-picking.
statement.notes: The function should not throw any exceptions.

hints.text: Check the length of the commit hash.
Use a regular expression to match the commit hash format.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Invalid Git Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation
editorial.intuition: The function should check the length of the commit hash and its format to determine if it's valid.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to iterate over all possible commit hashes and check if they match the expected format.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a regular expression to match the commit hash format.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match the commit hash format and check its length.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is to show that the regular expression matches all valid commit hashes and no invalid ones.
commonMistakes: Not checking the length of the commit hash
